issue_title: backward on 5090(sm_120) much slower than on 4090
surface_reasoning: Priority 3: labeled `module: performance` + `module: cuda`. Title: 'backward on 5090(sm_120) much slower than on 4090' — hardware-specific kernel performance anomaly on Blackwell (sm_120) vs Ada (sm_89). Domain relevance: backward pass on Blackwell architecture requires CUDA kernels compiled for sm_120; significant slowdown indicates missing sm_120 kernel specializations or wrong kernel dispatch for Blackwell.

maintainer_signals.author: eqy
maintainer_signals.quote: This is indeed due to the double precision kernel being much slower on 5090 rather than anything intrinsic to pytorch. I'll forward this to the cuBLAS team.

maintainer_signals.author: eqy
maintainer_signals.quote: the performance of FP64 is lower in the hardware but the support can be improved on the software side as well. Unfortunately it looks like the version of cuBLAS that includes this fix was too new to be picked up by PyTorch 2.9.

blocking_signals: maintainer_blocked_action: This is indeed due to the double precision kernel being much slower on 5090 rather than anything intrinsic to pytorch. I'll forward this to the cuBLAS team.
confidence_justification: Step 1: Start at 0.50. Step 2: 'triaged' label present (+0.10). Running total: 0.60. Step 3: Repro script present — fully self-contained script with forward/backward timing is in the issue body (+0.15). Running total: 0.75. Step 4: maintainer_blocked_action in blocking_signals — COLLABORATOR eqy explicitly stated 'This is indeed due to the double precision kernel being much slower on 5090 rather than anything intrinsic to pytorch'; root cause is in the cuBLAS library, not PyTorch, and a contributor cannot fix this without cuBLAS changes (-0.40). Running total: 0.35. Step 5: fix_requires_external_lib also applies — fix depends on a newer cuBLAS version that has not been picked up by PyTorch yet (eqy: 'the version of cuBLAS that includes this fix was too new to be picked up by PyTorch 2.9') (-0.30). Running total: 0.05. Step 6: Hard blocker present (maintainer_blocked_action) — force recommended_action='reject' regardless of confidence score. Step 7: No 'module: correctness (silent)' label. Final: clamp(0.05, 0.0, 1.0) = 0.05. Hard blocker forces reject routing.
ground_truth_delta: Very similar to ambiguous_72408: both have an explicit maintainer statement that the problem is not intrinsic to PyTorch and requires an external library fix (cuBLAS for 160838, integer BLAS for 72408). Key differences: (1) eqy's follow-up comment hints a cuBLAS fix already exists and could be picked up via a PyTorch cuBLAS version bump — this is slightly more actionable than 72408 where no external fix existed; (2) 160838 is hardware-specific to RTX 5090/Blackwell (sm_120), making it narrower in scope; (3) 160838 is only ~8 months old vs 72408's 4+ years; (4) despite these differences, the hard blocker (maintainer_blocked_action) makes both identical in routing — rejected.
